frameworks.name: EEG coherence
frameworks.one_line: Inter-electrode coherence indexes large-scale brain network connectivity during sleep.

frameworks.name: twin-design structural equation modeling (ACE)
frameworks.one_line: Decomposes coherence variance into additive genetic, shared- and unique-environment factors.

frameworks.name: thalamocortical sleep-spindle network
frameworks.one_line: Spindle activity treated as a marker of thalamocortical circuit organization.

core_question: How much do genetic versus environmental factors shape sleep EEG coherence in early adolescence?
key_assumptions: MZ vs DZ twin contrast isolates additive genetic variance
Sleep coherence is a stable trait-like marker of network connectivity
manipulations: Sleep state and EEG frequency band
key_findings: Unique environmental factors explain ~66% of coherence variance
Genetic factors contribute ~19% on average across frequencies/states
Sleep spindle coherence is an exception, with ~48% genetic contribution
Coherence shows high night-to-night intraindividual stability
interpretations: Sleep EEG coherence behaves as a trait-like phenotype despite modest heritability
Thalamocortical spindle networks are more genetically constrained than broader cortical coherence
